NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED EF1 TORNADO IN THE HAWS RUN AREA WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100 TO 110 MPH, A PATH LENGTH OF 5.5 MILES AND A PATH WIDTH OF 0.33 MILES. THE DAMAGE CONSISTED MAINLY OF LARGE TREES, BUT ALSO INCLUDED DAMAGE TO STRUCTURES.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND DAMAGE FROM MICROBURST (ESTIMATED WINDS 75-85 MPH) JUST EAST OF CONFIRMED TORNADO PATH. MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS WITHIN A SMALL RESIDENTIAL AREA ON CLAYTON JAMES ROAD, AND INCLUDED SEVERAL LARGE TREES EITHER SNAPPED OR TOPPLED OVER, ROOF DAMAGE TO A FEW MANUFACTURED HOMES, AND NUMEROUS LARGE LIMBS DOWN.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND TORNADO DAMAGE FROM HURRICANE ISAIAS IN WESTERN PAMLICO COUNTY. TREES WERE SNAPPED ALONG PINE LN IN KENNELS BEACH THAT FELL ON A HOME, AND ADDITIONAL SNAPPED TREES ALONG A RURAL LOGGING ROAD JUST NW OF KENNELS BEACH. THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO THE WESTERN END OF GOOSE CREEK RD., PRODUCING AN AREA OF ENHANCE SOFTWOOD AND HARDWOOD TREE DAMAGE. PATH LENGTH IS ESTIMATED AT 2.35 MILES, PATH WIDTH 40 YARDS, AND MAXIMUM INTENSITY OF 100 MPH (EF1).

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF-1 TORNADO ASSOCIATED WITH HURRICANE ISAIAS FORMED ON THE SOUTHEAST SIDE OF GOOSE CREEK STATE PARK THAT TRACKED NORTHEAST TOWARDS THE PARK’S PARKING LOT. A SWATH OF LARGE PINE TREES WERE DAMAGED ACROSS THE TRACK, SNAPPED FROM TWO TO TWENTY FEET UP, AND ACROSS TRAILS WITHIN THE STATE PARK. NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES WERE OBSERVED WITH THIS TORNADO. SPECIAL THANKS TO GOOSE CREEK STATE PARK FOR THEIR COORDINATION WITH THIS STORM SURVEY.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF-1 TORNADO IMPACTED RURAL BEAUFORT COUNTY NORTHEAST OF PINETOWN ASSOCIATED WITH HURRICANE ISAIAS. SEVERAL LARGE HARDWOOD TREES WERE UPROOTED AND LARGE LIMBS WERE SNAPPED ALONG HIGHWAY 32. DAMAGE WAS ALSO NOTED TO A LARGE CORN FIELD EAST OF HIGHWAY 32. THE TORNADO THEN MOVED NORTH-NORTHWEST INTO RURAL FORESTED AND HARD TO REACH RURAL AREAS OF BEAUFORT COUNTY. NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES WERE OBSERVED WITH THIS TORNADO.
STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF-1 TORNADO IN BAYVIEW, NC WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 105MPH, PATH WIDTH OF 180 YARDS, AND PATH LENGTH OF 0.80 MILES.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF-1 TORNADO ASSOCIATED WITH HURRICANE ISAIAS FORMED IN RURAL SOUTHEASTERN MARTIN COUNTY, UPROOTING A LARGE OAK TREE THAT FELL ON A MOBILE HOME. THE TORNADO PRODUCED ADDITIONAL TREE AND CROP DAMAGE EAST OF MANNING RD. AND ON THE NORTHWEST SIDE OF THE MOBILE HOME PARK ON MANNING RD. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 100 MPH, PATH WIDTH OF 40 YARDS, AND LENGTH OF 0.2 MILES. NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES WERE OBSERVED WITH THIS TORNADO.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND A BRIEF EF1 TORNADO IMPACTED MERRIMON. MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS WERE 90-100 MPH, WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 0.15 MILES AND MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 25 YARDS. NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES WERE OBSERVED WITH THIS TORNADO.

AN EF0 TORNADO OCCURRED ALONG HIGHWAY 33 WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 70 MPH. THE PATH WIDTH WAS 20 YARDS AND THE PATH LENGTH WAS 0.1 MILES. SMALLER LIMBS WERE FOUND ALONG AND OVER HIGHWAY 33. 1 LARGER PINE TREE WAS SNAPPED AND ANOTHER UPROOTED JUST EAST OF THE HIGHWAY. THE PATH LENGTH MAY HAVE BEEN LONGER BUT COULD NOT BE SURVEYED DUE TO THE REMOTE LOCATION.
